question: Spain defeated Argentina to win the FIFA World Cup 2026. At which stadium was the final match played?
detail:
- The Final of the **FIFA World Cup 2026** was played at the **MetLife Stadium** in the **State of New Jersey, USA**.
- **Spain** won the title of the **23rd FIFA World Cup 2026**, beating **Argentina 1–0 in Extra Time**.
- The only and decisive goal of the match was scored by **Ferran Torres** in the **106th minute**.
- This is **Spain's second FIFA World Cup title** — the first was in **2010**.
- France's **Kylian Mbappé** won the **Golden Boot Award** with **10 goals**, becoming the first player to win this honour in **two consecutive** FIFA World Cups.
- **Award winners:** Golden Ball (Best Player) — **Rodri (Spain)**; Silver Ball — **Lionel Messi (Argentina)**; Golden Boot (Top Scorer) — **Kylian Mbappé (10 goals)**; Golden Glove — **Unai Simón (Spain)**; FIFA Fair Play Trophy — **Netherlands**.
- **23rd FIFA World Cup (2026):** organised **11 June – 19 July 2026**; host countries — **USA, Canada and Mexico**; Champion — **Spain** (Captain **Rodrigo Hernández**); Runner-up — **Argentina** (Captain **Lionel Messi**).
- **Prize money:** Champion (Spain) — **USD 50 million** (about ₹477 crore); Runner-up (Argentina) — **USD 33 million** (about ₹314 crore).
- **About the FIFA World Cup:** the most prestigious international competition for men's national football teams, organised every **4 years** by **FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association)**. The first FIFA World Cup was in **1930 (Uruguay)**.
- **FIFA World Cup 2022:** Winner — **Argentina** (3rd title — 1978, 1986, 2022); Runner-up — **France**; held in **Qatar**.
- **About FIFA:** the international body for football. Founded **1904**; Founder — **Robert Guérin**; headquarters **Zurich, Switzerland**; President **Gianni Infantino**.

question: Which film won the Best Feature Film award at the 72nd National Film Awards?
detail:
- **Article 370**, directed by **Aditya Suhas Jambhale**, won the **Best Feature Film** award at the **72nd National Film Awards**.
- The awards were announced in **New Delhi** and were given for films certified by the **CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification)** in **2024**.
- **Major winners:** Best Non-Feature Film — **Bhangaar** (Marathi and English); Best Popular Film — **Kalki 2898 AD** (Director **Nag Ashwin**); Best Director — **Rajkumar Periasamy** (**Amaran**, Tamil).
- **Best Actor** — **Mammootty** (**Bramayugam**) and **Kartik Aaryan** (**Chandu Champion**); **Best Actress** — **Yami Gautam** (**Article 370**); **Best Debut Director** — **Randeep Hooda** (**Swatantrya Veer Savarkar**).
- **Regional films:** Best Hindi Film — **Srikanth**; Best Tamil Film — **Raayan**; Best Telugu Film — **Committee Kurrollu**; Best Malayalam Film — **Feminichi Fathima**; Best Kannada Film — **Mithya**.
- **About the National Film Awards:** given for the best performances in Indian cinema across different categories, and presented to the winners by the **President**. First award — **10 October 1954**; presented by the **National Film Development Corporation (NFDC)** since **2021**.
- **How the awards are given:** all categories are divided into **3 sections** — **Feature Film, Non-Feature Film and Best Writing on Cinema**. The **6 categories** of Feature Films, the **3 categories** of Non-Feature Films and Best Writing on Cinema are given the **Swarna Kamal (Golden Lotus Award)**, while all the rest are given the **Rajat Kamal (Silver Lotus Award)**.
- **Note:** a **Feature film** is a big film made for theatres, with a length of about **75 minutes or more**; a **Non-feature film** is a small film such as a documentary or short film.

question: Who has been appointed as the new Prime Minister of the United Kingdom (UK)?
detail:
- **Andy Burnham** took charge as the new **Prime Minister of the United Kingdom (UK)**. He replaced **Keir Starmer**.
- Keir Starmer had resigned from the post of **Prime Minister** and as leader of the **Labour Party**.
- The **Labour Party** chose Andy Burnham as its new leader — and in the UK the leader of the party that has a **majority in Parliament** becomes the Prime Minister.
- **About the United Kingdom:** official name — **United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland**; Capital — **London**; Currency — **Pound Sterling (GBP)**; Head of State (Monarch) — **King Charles III**.
- The UK is made up of **4 constituent countries**: **England** (Capital **London**), **Scotland** (Capital **Edinburgh**), **Wales** (Capital **Cardiff**) and **Northern Ireland** (Capital **Belfast**).

question: When is International Moon Day celebrated every year?
detail:
- **International Moon Day** is celebrated every year on **20 July**.
- The day is observed as the anniversary of humans setting foot on the **Moon** for the first time through the **Apollo 11** mission in **1969**.
- The day was recognised by the **UN in 2021** and was celebrated for the first time on **20 July 2022**.
- **2026 theme:** **"One Moon, One Vision, One Future"**.
- **About Apollo 11:** launched by **NASA**; mission duration — **8 days, 3 hours, 18 minutes, 35 seconds**; crew of **3** — **Neil A. Armstrong, Michael Collins and Edwin E. Aldrin Jr**.
- **Launch date** — **16 July 1969**; **Launch site** — **Kennedy, USA**; **Moon landing date** — **20 July 1969**.

question: Who became the first Indian to win the Japan Open Women's Singles title in 2026?
detail:
- **P. V. Sindhu** won the **Women's Singles** title of the **Japan Open 2026 (BWF Super 750)** by defeating Japan's **Akane Yamaguchi** in the final.
- The tournament was held at the **Tokyo Metropolitan Gymnasium, Tokyo (Japan)**.
- **Key points:** it was her **first Japan Open title** and her **first BWF Super 750 title**; it was the **sixth BWF World Tour title** of her career and her biggest title since the **2019 BWF World Championships**.
- **What is the Japan Open:** one of the biggest **badminton tournaments** in the world, held every year in **Japan**, organised by the **BWF (Badminton World Federation)**. It is a **BWF World Tour Super 750** level tournament.
- **About P. V. Sindhu:** full name **Pusarla Venkata Sindhu**; sport — **Badminton**; country — **India**; date of birth — **5 July 1995**.
- **Major achievements:** Olympics — **Silver (Rio 2016)** and **Bronze (Tokyo 2020)**; **BWF World Champion 2019** (first Indian to win Gold); **Commonwealth Games Gold** (Women's Singles, **2022**).

question: Who has been appointed as the Chairperson of the National Commission for Minorities (NCM)?
detail:
- **Harjit Singh Grewal** has been appointed **Chairperson** of the **National Commission for Minorities (NCM)**.
- His **tenure will be 3 years** from the date of taking charge.
- The post had been **vacant since April 2025**, after the tenure of **Iqbal Singh Lalpura** ended.
- **Munawari Begum** has been appointed as the **Vice-Chairperson** of the commission and **Glenn E. Souza Ticlo** as a **Member**. Munawari Begum was earlier a Member of the NCM and has now been made Vice-Chairperson.
- **About the NCM:** a **Statutory Body** of the Government of India whose work is to protect the rights of **minority communities** and take their problems to the government.
- It was constituted under the **National Commission for Minorities Act, 1992**.
- **Composition:** a **Chairperson**, a **Vice-Chairperson** and **5 Members** — **7 members in total**, all appointed by the **Central Government**.

question: India's first Urban Night Safari will be developed in which state?
detail:
- The **Supreme Court** has approved the **Uttar Pradesh Government's Kukrail Night Safari & Day Zoo Project**.
- With this, the way has been cleared for setting up **India's first** and the **world's 5th Night Safari**.
- The aim of the project is to promote **Tourism, Wildlife Conservation, Research, Education and Nature-based Tourism**.
- The project will be developed in the **Kukrail Reserve Forest** of **Lucknow (Uttar Pradesh)**, whose total area is **2,027 hectares**. Costing about **₹1,510 crore**, it will be developed in **two phases** over an area of about **346 hectares**.
- It was announced in **July 2022**.
- **What is a Night Safari:** a wildlife park where tourists can move around at night and see animals that are more active at night (**Nocturnal**).
- At present Night Safaris operate only in **Singapore, Thailand, China and Indonesia**.
- **About the Kukrail Reserve Forest:** developed as a **Plantation Forest in the 1950s**. It is named after the **Kukrail river**, a tributary of the **Gomti river**.

question: Where is the headquarters of the newly launched World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organisation (WAICO)?
detail:
- **China** has established the **World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization (WAICO)**, whose headquarters will be in **Shanghai (China)**.
- It is considered the world's **first intergovernmental international organization** in the field of **AI**.
- Its objective is to promote **international cooperation, Capacity Building** and an inclusive **global AI Governance Framework** in the field of AI.
- **29 countries** signed the agreement, including **Kazakhstan, Laos, Pakistan, Russia and Indonesia**.
- All these countries have become the **Founding Members** of WAICO.

question: Which state has become the first in India to introduce a Risk-based Business Approval System?
detail:
- The **Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly** passed the **Chhattisgarh Ease of Doing Business Act, 2026**.
- With this, **Chhattisgarh** became the **first state in India** to implement a **Risk-based and Trust-based Business Approval Framework**.
- Its objective is to simplify **business approvals**, increase **investment** and develop a fast, transparent and accountable approval system for **MSMEs**.
- **Major features:** under the Act a **Risk-based Approval System** has been implemented in which **low-risk businesses** get the facility of **Self-Certification** and **simplified compliance**, while regular **inspection** continues for **high-risk projects**.
- If an application is not disposed of within the fixed time limit, **Deemed (Auto) Approval** will be granted. The compulsion of **Annual Licence Renewal** has also been abolished.
- In the initial phase, **43 services of 8 departments** have been included, and more than **15 lakh (1.5 million) MSMEs** are likely to benefit from it.

question: Which state has been honoured with the Agriculture Leadership Award 2026 for Horticulture?
detail:
- **Gujarat** was honoured with the **Agriculture Leadership Award 2026** for outstanding performance in the field of **Horticulture**, at the **17th Agriculture Leadership Conclave-2026** in **New Delhi**.
- The award was presented by **Defence Minister Rajnath Singh** to Gujarat's Agriculture Minister **Jitu Vaghani**.
- **About the Agriculture Leadership Award:** given to states and institutions for outstanding performance and **innovation** in the **agriculture sector**. Introduced — **2008** (recommended by **M. S. Swaminathan**); presented by the **Agriculture Today Group**.
- **About the Agriculture Today Group (ATG):** a leading Indian **agri-media and knowledge platform** that provides information and publications and organises global events related to **Agriculture, Agribusiness, Policy, Research and Technique**. Headquarters — **New Delhi**; Founded — **2000**.
